reasons: H5 (narrow certified band for BN) strongly supported across CIFAR-10 (0.25% spread), CIFAR-100 (0.76%), VGG-16-BN (0.12%) with 7+ methods and 3 seeds each
No hypothesis falsified by pilot evidence — all core predictions remain viable
PMP-WD pilot confirms implementation convergence and bang-bang switching behavior; LR mismatch explains -0.39% delta vs constant baseline
Framework resilient to negative results: narrow band IS the finding, explaining why constant WD is near-optimal
Novelty score 7/10 with genuinely new contributions (Lyapunov certificate, PMP-WD optimality, formal subsumption)
Both backup candidates recommended by novelty report to merge into main candidate (novelty 5/10 each)
Random mask matching CWD on CIFAR-10 provides strong ablation evidence for the framework
next_actions: Fix PMP-WD LR from 0.001 to 0.1 to match baselines
Re-run PMP-WD pilot with corrected LR on CIFAR-10/ResNet-20
Execute full PMP-WD campaign: CIFAR-10 + CIFAR-100, 3 seeds each
Run instrumented reruns of 5 methods to validate H1 (certificate) and H3 (subsumption)
Complete NoBN ablation with all 8 methods x 3 seeds to properly test H5
Debug and execute ImageNet pilot before committing to full ImageNet campaign
Absorb alignment mirage thesis as H5 analysis section in the paper
key_risks: PMP-WD may still underperform constant WD with matched LR — reframe as 'constant is near-optimal' (still publishable)
Instrumented reruns may show certified band too narrow for meaningful subsumption analysis
ImageNet pipeline has failed in prior iterations — needs careful debugging
Cumulative alignment bound (H2) may not improve on worst-case — lowest priority hypothesis
